title: dupatya pedunculata
intro: Dupatya pedunculata, also known as the Indian gentian or gentianella, is a plant species within the Gentianaceae family that is native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. It is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Dupatya pedunculata is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
